shelves: Shelves line both walls, floor to ceiling
Shelves on every surface, packed tight
The shelves rise above you on both sides
Rows and rows of identical shelves
Shelves from floor to ceiling, unbroken
The walls are shelves, the shelves are walls
Dark wood shelves, every inch filled
Shelf after shelf, each one the same height
The shelves press in from both sides
Books packed into shelves that reach the ceiling
The shelves are deep and full
books: Calfskin spines packed tight on every shelf
Every book the same size, the same binding
Thousands of books, none distinguishable from the next
The spines are uniform, gold-edged, unmarked
Books. More books. The same books
Gold leaf on calfskin, repeated without end
Each spine the same muted brown
The books are indifferent to your attention
Row after row of gilded spines
Nothing on the spines. No titles, no authors
floor: Your footsteps vanish into the carpet
The carpet is the same deep red it always is
The floor gives slightly with each step
Red carpet, worn but not threadbare
The carpet has no stains, no wear marks
Your feet make no sound on the carpet
The pile of the carpet is uniform and deep
Carpet the color of dried blood, immaculate
ahead: The corridor continues in both directions without variation
More of the same in every direction
Another gallery, same as the last
The next section is indistinguishable from this one
You can see a long way. Nothing changes
The vanishing point is the same in both directions
silence: No sound but your own breathing
The stillness presses against your ears
Quiet. The kind that has weight
The air is still and has been still forever
The quiet is a physical thing here
You could hear a page turn from three galleries away
observation: You've been here before. Or somewhere exactly like here
There is no way to tell this gallery from the last
If you turned around, you would not know the difference
The monotony is structural, not incidental
Nothing distinguishes this stretch from any other
You check the shelf nearest you. Calfskin. Gold edges. Of course
Every surface is maintained. By what, you cannot say
The light comes from everywhere and nowhere
No markers. No signs. No graffiti that lasts
This could be any gallery on any floor on any side
sameness: The same as the last. The same as the next
Everything here is the same as everything else
You have been walking for a long time and nothing has changed
Variation is not a feature of this place
If you looked at this gallery a thousand years from now it would be identical
The library does not change. You do
There is no landmark. There is no need for one

light: The light barely reaches the lower shelves
Something wrong with the lights in this section
The overhead light stutters and holds at half
Dimmer here. Not dark, but diminished
The light is thin and reluctant
shadows: The shelf-shadows are deep enough to lose things in
The gaps between shelves are black
Your shadow stretches long and vague
The lower shelves disappear into murk
effect: The spines are harder to make out
The sameness is harder to perceive, which is almost a relief

rest: A rest area, same as all the others
The rest area opens up around you
A break in the shelves — the rest area
Rest area. You recognize the layout without looking
clock: A clock, showing the same time as every other clock
The clock face is readable from here
kiosk: The kiosk glows in its alcove
The soft hum of the kiosk
beds: A row of seven beds behind a partition
Seven beds, thin mattresses, metal frames
The bedroom partition is open. Seven beds, as always
slot: A submission slot set into the stone
The submission slot gleams in the wall
The brass submission slot, slightly worn
A slot in the wall. Brass edges

liedown: You pick a bed. It doesn't matter which
You collapse onto the nearest mattress
You stretch out on the thin bed
You choose a bed and lie still
mattress: The mattress is the same as every other mattress
The sheets are clean. They are always clean
The pillow is flat and cool
Metal frame, thin pad, white sheet
The bed is not comfortable. It is sufficient
unconscious: The darkness behind your eyelids is no different from the darkness of the library
You dream of nothing. You always dream of nothing
Unconsciousness arrives like a door closing
You are asleep before you finish the thought
The strange mercy of unconsciousness in a place with no mercy
silence: The silence is absolute — not even the whisper of air movement
In the dark, there is nothing to distinguish this bed from any other
The other beds are empty. Or occupied. You can't tell and don't check

sleep_mood_low: You wonder if tomorrow will be different. You know it won't.
Tomorrow you'll do this again. And the day after.
You try to remember something good. It takes a long time.
The weight of the days sits on your chest.
sleep_mood_despairing: You don't want to wake up. You will anyway.
Sleep is the closest thing to escape this place offers.
You can't remember what hope felt like.
There is nothing waiting for you tomorrow. There is nothing waiting for you ever.
You close your eyes and wish they wouldn't open again.
The thought of standing up tomorrow is unbearable. You'll do it anyway.

liedown: You sit down against the shelves and close your eyes
You curl up on the carpet
You lie down where you stand
You lower yourself to the floor
You stop walking and let your legs fold
surface: The carpet is thin over stone
The floor is not comfortable. It is what there is
The carpet smells faintly of dust and nothing else
You use your arm as a pillow
The shelves press against your back
unconscious: The darkness behind your eyelids is no different from the darkness of the library
You dream of nothing. You always dream of nothing
Unconsciousness arrives like a door closing
You are asleep before you finish the thought
The strange mercy of unconsciousness in a place with no mercy
silence: The silence is absolute — not even the whisper of air movement
In the dark, the corridor is indistinguishable from any other

water: Clear water, no glass, no ceremony
The water is perfect. It is always perfect
relief: You'd forgotten how bad it had gotten
Your body remembers what it needs even when you forget
The relief is immediate and temporary
aftermath: The only thing here that feels real
It tastes like nothing. That's fine
You drink until you stop wanting to
The cup disappears when you set it down

eat: You asked for something specific. It arrives exactly right
taste: It tastes exactly like what you asked for. That's almost worse
Perfect. Uncanny. Like a memory made solid
It tastes the way food tasted when you were alive
aftermath: The hunger fades. Everything else remains
The memory of the meal will outlast the satisfaction
You feel better. That's all it does

drink: The alcohol is exact — whatever you imagined, delivered
Something strong. It doesn't matter what
You asked for something you used to like
effect: For a moment, the shelves seem less oppressive
Your shoulders drop. Your jaw unclenches
The library blurs at the margins
fade: Addiction doesn't stick here — just the temporary mercy of it
Tomorrow you won't even remember what you ordered
The clarity will return. It always does
